headline: CHILE COPPER AT RISK: China's Sulphuric Acid Ban Takes Effect May 1 — 200Kt Output Threatened
snippet: China's ban on sulphuric acid exports kicked in May 1, directly threatening Chilean copper production — Chile imports over one million tonnes of Chinese sulphuric acid annually, and the shutdown (compounded by Middle East war disrupting sulphur shipments) puts an estimated 200,000 tonnes of copper output at immediate risk. Combined with the ongoing Grasberg force majeure in Indonesia, analysts now forecast a 2026 refined copper deficit of up to 600,000 tonnes.

headline: SILVER SURGES to $76/oz as Dollar Stumbles on Japan Intervention — Up 138% Year-Over-Year
snippet: Silver climbed above $76/oz on May 1, up 3.47% on the session, as a sharp US dollar depreciation following Japan's reported currency market intervention lifted precious metals broadly. Silver is now up 138% year-over-year, supported by structural industrial deficits tied to solar manufacturing and AI hardware demand.

headline: NGEX MINERALS: 48m at 7.72% CuEq Plus New Zone Discovered 60m From Surface at Lunahuasi
snippet: NGEx Minerals' Phase 4 drilling at Lunahuasi in San Juan, Argentina returned 34m at 7.25% CuEq and 48m at 7.72% CuEq, 180 metres apart in the Mars zone, plus a brand-new high-grade structure 300m east of Mars returning 23m at 4.72% CuEq from just 60m depth. Phase 5 drilling is planned for Q3 2026, and an exploration adit is under construction to enable underground drill access.

headline: ABRASILVER DRILLS 917 g/t Silver Core: 68.5m at 343 g/t at Argentina's Diablillos
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. (TSX-V: ABRA) intersected 68.5 metres grading 343 g/t silver in Phase VI drilling at Diablillos, Argentina, including a 17-metre core at 917 g/t silver along the JAC-Oculto trend. The result confirms high-grade continuity at the wholly-owned advanced-stage silver-gold project, which is advancing toward a feasibility study.

headline: RADISSON GOES DEEP: O'Brien Gold Extends to 1.9km — 4.54 g/t Au Over 12m at Record Depth
snippet: Radisson Mining (TSX-V: RDS) extended high-grade gold mineralization at the O'Brien Gold Project in Quebec's Abitibi belt to 1.9km vertical depth, with the project's deepest-ever hole returning 4.54 g/t gold over 12m. Seven rigs are running a 140,000m step-out campaign against a current inferred resource of 1.69Moz at 5.08 g/t gold.

headline: Gold Retreats to $4,571 Despite Tightening Iran Blockade — Still Up 41% From a Year Ago
snippet: Gold fell 1.12% to $4,571/oz on May 1 as traders took profits despite a tightening US naval blockade of Iran and ongoing Middle East uncertainty. The metal remains 41% higher year-over-year, with institutional consensus price targets ranging from $4,500 to $5,100/oz for H1 2026.

headline: World Bank WARNS: Gold and Silver Face a Price Ceiling Through the Rest of 2026
snippet: The World Bank issued a cautionary forecast arguing that structural limits on precious metals demand could cap gold and silver prices through the rest of 2026, even amid ongoing geopolitical volatility. The report presents a contrarian view to the broad institutional consensus calling for $5,000+ gold by year-end.

headline: Rare Earth Mining Poisoning Mekong Tributaries, Threatening Millions Who Depend on River
snippet: Toxic runoff from rare earth mining operations is contaminating Mekong River tributaries, threatening the farming and fisheries livelihoods of millions across Southeast Asia. The investigation highlights mounting ESG and regulatory liability risk for companies operating or investing in rare earth projects in the region.

headline: EMPEROR METALS Builds 1.46Moz Quebec Gold Resource After 23,000m Drill Campaign
snippet: Emperor Metals Inc. delivered a 1.46Moz gold resource estimate at Duquesne West in Quebec after a 23,000m drill campaign that intersected visible gold. The TSX-V junior now holds a significant resource in the Abitibi gold belt at a moment when senior producers are aggressively pursuing development-stage acquisitions.

headline: Mining M&A Hits $21.6B — Strongest Q1 in Three Years as Gold Sector Eyes Consolidation
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026 — the strongest start to a year since 2023 — according to White and Case. Precious metals ranked as the most likely next consolidation target, with record gold prices generating free cash flow and elevated premiums on advanced development projects.

headline: CORE LITHIUM Fires Up Finniss Mine This Month — A$290M Secured, Glencore Partnership Signed
snippet: Core Lithium greenlighted the restart of Australia's Finniss lithium mine with A$290M in backing from Glencore, InfraVia, and Nebari Natural Resources, awarding a A$50M mining contract to NRW for ore extraction beginning this month. First spodumene concentrate shipment is targeted for Q4 2026 — the first tangible signal that the battered Australian lithium sector may be turning.

headline: China's Rare Earth Export Controls Confirmed PERMANENT — Terbium, Dysprosium, Yttrium Locked In
snippet: Legal analysts confirmed that China's Announcement 18 rare earth export controls are now permanently embedded in the country's dual-use control framework, covering terbium, dysprosium, samarium, yttrium, and other medium and heavy rare earths. With no suspension granted, companies in defense, EV, and electronics supply chains continue to face ongoing licensing obligations with no end date.
